title: I am an investor
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for claiming the identity of someone who puts money to work.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 40 of the MONEY program. For when the word 'investor' still describes someone else rather than you.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 40 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Investing isn't for others. It's for you."

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 40 of the MONEY program. The identity claim is the active element: I am, not I want to be.
I am an investor. I put my money to work. Money grows in my hands.
Say it three times in the morning. On the third pass, picture a specific account or holding: not the size it needs to be, but the act of it existing and growing.
Adapted from Day 40 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
